case_name: ARMANDO PAYAS v. ADVENTIST HEALTH SYSTEM

summary.brief: ARMANDO PAYAS v. ADVENTIST HEALTH SYSTEM. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: In July 2009, Dr. Parra-Davila performed a paraesophageal hernia repair surgery on Mr. Galarza using a surgical robot.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During the surgery, part of the surgical robot detached and became embedded in Mr. Galarza's esophagus.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A second surgery was performed by a different surgeon at Tampa General Hospital in July 2012 to determine the cause of his worsening symptoms, but the surgery was unsuccessful.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In January 2013, a third, exploratory surgery was performed at Tampa General Hospital. During that surgery, Mr. Galarza's vena cava vein was ruptured, causing considerable blood loss, cardiac arrest, and ultimately his death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: An autopsy revealed the presence of a thin, coiled band encircling the gastroesophageal junction.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Payas alleged that this foreign object was part of the surgical robot that became detached during the first surgery in July 2009.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court erred in dismissing the fourth complaint against Celebration Health with prejudice because it contains sufficient allegations for causes of action for vicarious liability, breach of a nondelegable duty, and negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We reverse the order dismissing counts I, II, IV, and V with prejudice and remand for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Complications following paraesophageal hernia repair surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr. Galarza underwent paraesophageal hernia repair surgery using a surgical robot. During the surgery, part of the robot detached and became embedded in his esophagus, leading to complications.

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan of the abdomen and thorax

reveal.result_summary: Foreign object observed in the esophagus
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates presence of a foreign object that may be causing complications
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt surgical intervention to remove the object

timeline_phases.name: Initial Surgery
timeline_phases.description: Paraesophageal hernia repair surgery using a surgical robot

key_events.event: Surgical robot part detached and embedded in esophagus

timeline_phases.name: Post-Surgical Complications
timeline_phases.description: Patient experiences complications and undergoes further evaluation

timeline_phases.name: Management Decision
timeline_phases.description: Decision on how to manage the detected foreign object

key_events.event: Decision to monitor rather than remove foreign object

decision_points.clinical_context: Post-surgical complications with foreign object detected
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Perform exploratory surgery to remove the foreign object
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to remove the foreign object and prevent further complications

options.description: Monitor the patient and manage symptoms conservatively
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate as it does not address the underlying issue of the foreign object

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant chose to monitor the patient

expected_action_court.description: Exploratory surgery should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established the need for surgical intervention

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to remove the foreign object led to further complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Surgical removal of the foreign object
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered cardiac arrest and died following complications from the foreign object

key_findings: Court reversed the dismissal of the complaint, allowing further proceedings

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Removal of the object could have prevented complications

factual_timeline.event: Exploratory surgery performed, patient suffers cardiac arrest

court_findings.finding: Reversal of dismissal, allowing further proceedings
